prompt: Who is the all-time top scorer in UEFA Champions League history (including European Cup era)?
explanation: Cristiano Ronaldo is the competition's all-time top scorer with well over 140 goals.

prompt: Which goalkeeper holds the record for most clean sheets in Champions League history?
explanation: Iker Casillas holds the record for most Champions League clean sheets.

prompt: Which country won the first FIFA World Cup held outside of the Americas or Europe, in 2002?
explanation: Brazil won the 2002 World Cup, which was co-hosted by Japan and South Korea, the first held in Asia.

prompt: Who scored West Germany's controversial goal-line incident opponent in the 1966 World Cup Final, the disputed 'Wembley goal'?
explanation: Geoff Hurst's shot that bounced down off the crossbar was awarded a goal in the 1966 final, a lasting controversy.

prompt: Which player is the only one to score a hat-trick in a World Cup final?
explanation: Geoff Hurst scored a hat-trick for England in the 1966 World Cup final, still the only one ever.

prompt: Which club did Ferenc Puskas play for after leaving Hungary following the 1956 revolution?
explanation: Puskas joined Real Madrid in 1958 and formed a legendary partnership with Alfredo Di Stefano.

prompt: Who holds the record for most European Cup/Champions League goals scored by a single player in one final?
options: Alfredo Di Stefano and Ferenc Puskas both scored in the 1960 final
explanation: Puskas scored four and Di Stefano three in Real Madrid's 7-3 win over Eintracht Frankfurt in 1960.

prompt: What was the score of the legendary 1960 European Cup Final won by Real Madrid?
explanation: Real Madrid beat Eintracht Frankfurt 7-3 at Hampden Park, widely regarded as one of the greatest finals ever.

prompt: Which player scored the fastest goal in World Cup history, within 11 seconds?
explanation: Turkey's Hakan Sukur scored against South Korea in 2002 after just 10.8 seconds, a World Cup record.

prompt: Who is the youngest player to score in a FIFA World Cup Final?
explanation: Pele was 17 years old when he scored twice in the 1958 World Cup final.

prompt: Which team suffered the biggest defeat in World Cup history, losing 9-0?
explanation: Hungary beat El Salvador 10-1 in 1982 — the actual record. However, notable heavy defeats also include Yugoslavia 9-0 Zaire in 1974.

prompt: Which country holds the record for the biggest win margin in a FIFA World Cup match, beating El Salvador 10-1 in 1982?
explanation: Hungary beat El Salvador 10-1 at the 1982 World Cup, the largest victory margin in tournament history.

prompt: Who was the first player sent off in a World Cup final?
explanation: Argentina's Pedro Monzon was the first player red-carded in a World Cup final, in 1990 against West Germany.

prompt: Which two players were both sent off in the 1990 World Cup Final?
options: Two Argentine players, Monzon and Dezotti
explanation: Argentina's Pedro Monzon and Gustavo Dezotti were both sent off during the 1990 final, a first in World Cup final history.

prompt: Who scored the fastest hat-trick in Premier League history, in around 2 minutes 56 seconds?
explanation: Sadio Mane scored a hat-trick for Southampton against Aston Villa in 2015, the Premier League's fastest ever.

prompt: Which defender is often cited as having recorded the most interceptions of any player at UEFA Euro 2016?
explanation: Portugal's Pepe was among the tournament leaders for defensive actions at Euro 2016, which Portugal went on to win.

prompt: Who is the oldest player to score in a World Cup match?
explanation: Cameroon's Roger Milla scored at the 1994 World Cup aged 42, making him the oldest World Cup goal scorer.

prompt: Which goalkeeper is the oldest to play a match at a FIFA World Cup?
explanation: Egypt's Essam El-Hadary played at the 2018 World Cup aged 45, the oldest player ever to feature at a World Cup.

prompt: Which club did Alfredo Di Stefano famously refuse to sign for outright, leading to a unique dual-registration transfer between Barcelona and Real Madrid in 1953?
options: Di Stefano was jointly registered before ultimately playing for Real Madrid
He signed only for Real Madrid without dispute
explanation: A dispute over Di Stefano's transfer from River Plate/Millonarios saw him play preseason for both Barcelona and Real Madrid before settling with Madrid.

prompt: Which player has the most appearances in FIFA World Cup history?
explanation: Lionel Messi surpassed Lothar Matthaus's record, reaching 26 World Cup appearances by 2022.

prompt: Before Messi, who held the record for most FIFA World Cup appearances?
explanation: Germany's Lothar Matthaus made 25 World Cup appearances across five tournaments (1982-1998).

prompt: Which manager has won the most UEFA Champions League/European Cup titles?
explanation: Carlo Ancelotti has won a record five Champions League titles as a manager.

prompt: Which club did Carlo Ancelotti win his first Champions League title with as a manager?
explanation: Ancelotti won the Champions League with AC Milan in 2003 and 2007.

prompt: Who scored the only goal of the 1966 World Cup Final's controversial third goal for England, later disputed by West Germany fans for decades?
explanation: Hurst's shot hitting the crossbar and bouncing on the line was awarded, forming England's controversial third goal in a 4-2 win.

prompt: Which player holds the record for most goals scored in a single Champions League season?
explanation: Ronaldo scored a record 17 Champions League goals in the 2013-14 season for Real Madrid.

prompt: Which nation's team is nicknamed the 'Atlas Lions'?
explanation: Morocco's national team is nicknamed the 'Atlas Lions' after the Atlas mountain range.

prompt: Who was the first African player to win the Ballon d'Or?
explanation: Liberia's George Weah won the Ballon d'Or in 1995, the only African winner to date.

prompt: Which club did George Weah play for when he won the 1995 Ballon d'Or?
explanation: Weah won the Ballon d'Or in 1995 while playing for Paris Saint-Germain.

prompt: Which country's team lost the 1950 World Cup Final match known as the 'Maracanazo' to Uruguay?
explanation: Brazil lost the deciding match 2-1 to Uruguay in the 1950 tournament, a shock still remembered as the 'Maracanazo'.

prompt: Who scored the winning goal for Uruguay in the 1950 'Maracanazo' final match against Brazil?
explanation: Alcides Ghiggia scored the decisive goal in Uruguay's shock 2-1 win over Brazil in front of nearly 200,000 fans.

prompt: Which club won five consecutive European Cups from 1956 to 1960?
explanation: Real Madrid won the first five European Cup titles ever contested, from 1956 to 1960.

prompt: Which player top-scored in the European Cup for five consecutive seasons during Real Madrid's 1950s dominance?
explanation: Alfredo Di Stefano was the competition's top scorer for five consecutive seasons in the late 1950s.

prompt: Which player made the most appearances for Real Madrid in the European Cup during their five consecutive titles from 1956-1960?
explanation: Francisco Gento was part of all five of Real Madrid's consecutive European Cup wins.

prompt: Which nation won the first African Cup of Nations in 1957?
explanation: Egypt won the first Africa Cup of Nations in 1957, a tournament with just three teams.

prompt: Which country has won the most Africa Cup of Nations titles?
explanation: Egypt has won a record seven Africa Cup of Nations titles.

prompt: Who is the all-time top scorer for the Netherlands national team?
explanation: Robin van Persie is the Netherlands' all-time leading goal scorer.

prompt: Which player scored a famous long-range header against Spain at the 2014 World Cup for the Netherlands?
explanation: Van Persie's diving header helped the Netherlands beat defending champions Spain 5-1.

prompt: What was the final score when the Netherlands beat defending champions Spain at the 2014 World Cup?
explanation: The Netherlands stunned Spain 5-1 in a group stage match at the 2014 World Cup.

prompt: Which goalkeeper saved a crucial penalty from Ghana in stoppage time of the 2010 World Cup quarter-final, after handling the ball on the line himself?
options: Asamoah Gyan missed it himself off the crossbar
explanation: In the Uruguay-Ghana quarter-final, Luis Suarez handled on the line and Asamoah Gyan hit the resulting penalty off the crossbar.

prompt: Which player deliberately handled the ball on the goal line to deny Ghana a certain goal at the 2010 World Cup quarter-final?
explanation: Luis Suarez's handball prevented a Ghana goal; he was sent off but Uruguay progressed after the resulting penalty was missed.

prompt: Which club did Johan Cruyff manage to European Cup glory in 1992?
explanation: Cruyff managed Barcelona's 'Dream Team' to their first European Cup title in 1992.

prompt: Which club is nicknamed after the term 'Dream Team' during Johan Cruyff's managerial reign in the early 1990s?
explanation: Cruyff's Barcelona side of the early 1990s became known as the 'Dream Team'.

prompt: Which player scored the winning goal for Barcelona in the 1992 European Cup Final against Sampdoria?
explanation: Ronald Koeman's extra-time free kick won Barcelona their first European Cup in 1992.

prompt: Which country's national team went undefeated through an entire domestic season, nicknamed 'The Invincibles', in English football?
explanation: Preston North End were unbeaten in the first Football League season, and Arsenal repeated the feat in the Premier League era in 2003-04.

prompt: Who managed Arsenal's 'Invincibles' season in 2003-04?
explanation: Arsene Wenger managed Arsenal through their unbeaten 2003-04 Premier League season.

prompt: Which club has never been relegated from the Premier League since its founding in 1992?
options: Arsenal, Chelsea, Liverpool, Manchester United and Tottenham
explanation: Arsenal, Chelsea, Liverpool, Manchester United, Tottenham and Everton (until 2025) were the ever-present Premier League clubs for much of the league's history.

prompt: Which club won the very first Premier League title in the 1992-93 season?
explanation: Manchester United won the inaugural Premier League title in 1992-93 under Alex Ferguson.

prompt: Which manager won the Premier League title with Blackburn Rovers in 1995?
explanation: Kenny Dalglish led Blackburn Rovers to a surprise Premier League title in 1995, backed by Jack Walker's investment.

prompt: Which two players formed Blackburn's title-winning strike partnership, nicknamed the 'SAS', in 1994-95?
explanation: Alan Shearer and Chris Sutton formed the famous 'SAS' partnership for Blackburn's title win.

prompt: Which nation's men's team won consecutive Olympic gold medals in football in 1924 and 1928?
explanation: Uruguay won consecutive Olympic football gold medals in 1924 and 1928, cementing their early football dominance.

prompt: Which country won gold in men's Olympic football at the 1992 Barcelona Games?
explanation: Hosts Spain won men's Olympic football gold in 1992.

prompt: Which player has scored the most goals in a calendar year, according to widely cited records (91 goals in 2012)?
explanation: Messi scored a record 91 goals for club and country in the 2012 calendar year.

prompt: Which player previously held the record for most goals in a calendar year before Messi surpassed it in 2012?
explanation: Gerd Muller's 85-goal calendar year record from 1972 stood for four decades before Messi broke it.

prompt: Which club did Eusebio play for during his prime, becoming known as the 'Black Panther'?
explanation: Eusebio starred for Benfica and Portugal, winning the 1965 Ballon d'Or.

prompt: Which country did Eusebio represent, famously reaching the 1966 World Cup semi-final?
explanation: Eusebio was born in Mozambique but represented Portugal, top-scoring at the 1966 World Cup.

prompt: Which club won a continental treble (league, cup, and Champions League) in the 1998-99 season?
explanation: Manchester United won the Premier League, FA Cup, and Champions League in 1998-99.

prompt: Which club became the first in Spanish football to win a continental treble, doing so in 2009 under Pep Guardiola?
explanation: Barcelona won La Liga, the Copa del Rey and the Champions League in Guardiola's first season, 2008-09.

prompt: Which club is the only one to have won a continental sextuple (six trophies) in a single calendar year, achieved in 2009?
explanation: Barcelona won six trophies in 2009 under Guardiola, an unprecedented achievement.

prompt: Which club matched Barcelona's sextuple achievement in 2020?
explanation: Bayern Munich won six trophies in 2020, matching Barcelona's 2009 achievement.

prompt: Which manager led Bayern Munich to their 2020 sextuple?
explanation: Hansi Flick took over mid-season and guided Bayern to an unprecedented six trophies in 2020.

statement: Geoff Hurst scored the only hat-trick ever recorded in a FIFA World Cup Final.
explanation: Hurst's 1966 hat-trick for England remains the only World Cup final hat-trick in history.

statement: Real Madrid won the first five European Cup competitions ever held, from 1956 to 1960.
explanation: Real Madrid's dominance in the competition's early years remains unmatched.

statement: Luis Suarez was sent off for a deliberate handball against Ghana at the 2010 World Cup.
explanation: Suarez's handball on the line prevented a certain Ghana goal in the final minute of extra time.

statement: George Weah is the only African-born player to win the Ballon d'Or.
explanation: Weah, born in Liberia, remains the only African Ballon d'Or winner as of the mid-2020s.

statement: Uruguay beat host nation Brazil in the deciding match of the 1950 World Cup.
explanation: The 'Maracanazo' saw Uruguay beat Brazil 2-1 in front of a huge crowd in Rio de Janeiro.

statement: Lionel Messi's record of 91 goals in a calendar year was set in 2012.
explanation: Messi scored 91 goals for Barcelona and Argentina across the 2012 calendar year.

statement: Barcelona won six trophies in a single calendar year in 2009, a feat later matched by Bayern Munich in 2020.
explanation: Both clubs achieved unprecedented sextuples in their respective years.

statement: Egypt has won more Africa Cup of Nations titles than any other country.
explanation: Egypt's seven AFCON titles is a record.

statement: Arsenal's 'Invincibles' season occurred during the 2004-05 season.
explanation: Arsenal's unbeaten Premier League season was 2003-04, not 2004-05.

statement: Cristiano Ronaldo holds the record for most goals in a single Champions League season, with 17 in 2013-14.
explanation: Ronaldo's 17-goal haul in 2013-14 for Real Madrid remains a Champions League record.

statement: Essam El-Hadary is the oldest player to appear at a FIFA World Cup.
explanation: El-Hadary played for Egypt at the 2018 World Cup aged 45 years and 161 days.

statement: The 1990 World Cup Final saw two players from the same team sent off.
explanation: Argentina's Pedro Monzon and Gustavo Dezotti were both dismissed in the final against West Germany.

statement: Alfredo Di Stefano was the European Cup's top scorer for five consecutive seasons.
explanation: Di Stefano's scoring dominance helped Real Madrid win five straight European Cups.

statement: Roger Milla is the oldest player to score a goal at a FIFA World Cup.
explanation: Milla scored for Cameroon at the 1994 World Cup at age 42.

statement: Blackburn Rovers won the Premier League title in 1995 under manager Kenny Dalglish.
explanation: Blackburn's title win, backed by Jack Walker's funding, remains one of the league's biggest surprises.
Real Madrid's 7-3 win at Hampden Park is considered one of the greatest finals ever, with Puskas scoring four.

fixture: FIFA World Cup Group Stage 2014 — Netherlands vs Spain
explanation: The Netherlands stunned defending champions Spain 5-1, with Van Persie's diving header a highlight.

fixture: FIFA World Cup 1982 — Hungary vs El Salvador
explanation: Hungary's 10-1 win remains the largest victory margin in World Cup history.
Uruguay's shock win over hosts Brazil is remembered as the 'Maracanazo'.

fixture: UEFA Champions League Quarter-Final 2020 — Bayern Munich vs Barcelona
explanation: Bayern's 8-2 demolition of Barcelona remains one of the biggest shocks in Champions League history.
England won their only World Cup title with Geoff Hurst's hat-trick in extra time.

Part of the legendary 'Mighty Magyars' team of the 1950s
Later starred for Real Madrid after 1956
Scored four goals in the 1960 European Cup final
explanation: Puskas was one of the greatest forwards of the 1950s, starring for both Hungary and Real Madrid.

Nicknamed the 'Blond Arrow'
Legendary Real Madrid striker of the 1950s
Top scorer of the European Cup for five straight seasons
explanation: Di Stefano was the star of Real Madrid's five consecutive European Cup wins from 1956-1960.

Nicknamed the 'Black Panther'
Top scorer at the 1966 World Cup
Won the 1965 Ballon d'Or with Benfica
explanation: Eusebio was Benfica and Portugal's greatest star of the 1960s.

First and only African winner of the Ballon d'Or (1995)
Played for Monaco, PSG, AC Milan and Chelsea

explanation: Weah went on to become President of Liberia after his football career.

clues: Uruguayan winger
Scored the winning goal in the 1950 World Cup final
Nicknamed 'El Atomico'
Part of Uruguay's shock win over Brazil
explanation: Ghiggia's goal silenced the Maracana and won Uruguay the 1950 World Cup.

clues: German striker
Held the calendar-year scoring record before Messi
Legendary Bayern Munich and West Germany forward
Nicknamed 'Der Bomber'
explanation: Muller's prolific scoring for Bayern Munich and West Germany made him one of history's greatest strikers.

clues: Uruguayan forward
Handled the ball on the goal line at the 2010 World Cup
Later starred for Liverpool and Barcelona

explanation: Suarez's controversial handball against Ghana became one of the most talked-about incidents in World Cup history.

prompt: Order these European Cup/Champions League dynasties by their starting year (earliest first).
events: Real Madrid's five consecutive titles begin (1956)
explanation: Order: Real Madrid 1956, Ajax 1971, AC Milan 1989, Real Madrid 2016.

prompt: Order these football scoring records chronologically by the year they were set (earliest first).
events: Just Fontaine sets the single-World Cup goal record (1958)
Gerd Muller sets the calendar-year goal record (1972)
Lionel Messi breaks the calendar-year goal record (2012)
Cristiano Ronaldo sets the Champions League single-season goal record (2014)
explanation: Order: Fontaine's 1958 World Cup record, Muller's 1972 calendar record, Messi's 2012 record, then Ronaldo's 2014 Champions League record.

prompt: Order these World Cup hosting milestones chronologically (earliest first).
events: First World Cup held in Uruguay (1930)
First World Cup held in Asia, co-hosted by Japan and South Korea (2002)
First World Cup held in Africa, in South Africa (2010)
First World Cup held in the Middle East, in Qatar (2022)
explanation: Order: Uruguay 1930, Japan/South Korea 2002, South Africa 2010, Qatar 2022.

prompt: Which player scored the only goal as West Germany won the 1974 World Cup final against the Netherlands?
explanation: Gerd Muller scored the winner as West Germany beat the Netherlands 2-1 in the 1974 final.

prompt: Which Dutch player scored directly from the kickoff sequence in the 1974 World Cup final, only for West Germany to come back and win?
explanation: Johan Neeskens scored an early penalty for the Netherlands before West Germany recovered to win 2-1.

prompt: Which club knocked out holders Liverpool in the 2004-05 Champions League? (trick question warm-up) Actually — which club did Liverpool beat in the famous 2005 'Miracle of Istanbul' final?
explanation: Liverpool beat AC Milan on penalties after coming back from 3-0 down in the 2005 final.

prompt: Who scored a hat-trick for AC Milan in the first half of the 2005 Champions League final before Liverpool's comeback?
options: Andriy Shevchenko scored one; Hernan Crespo scored two
explanation: Milan led 3-0 at half-time through Maldini, and two from Crespo, before Liverpool's remarkable comeback.

prompt: Which goalkeeper made a series of crucial saves, including one legendary double-save from Shevchenko, in the 2005 Champions League final?
explanation: Jerzy Dudek's saves, including in the penalty shootout, were key to Liverpool's 2005 triumph.

prompt: Who captained West Germany to victory in the 1974 World Cup, and later managed them to the 1990 title?
explanation: Franz Beckenbauer is one of only three men to win the World Cup as both captain and manager.

prompt: Which club did Paolo Maldini spend his entire 25-year professional career at?
explanation: Maldini played over 900 matches for AC Milan across a single-club career from 1985 to 2009.

statement: Franz Beckenbauer won the World Cup as both a captain and a manager with West Germany/Germany.
explanation: Beckenbauer captained West Germany to the 1974 title and managed them to victory in 1990.

statement: Paolo Maldini played his entire senior career for a single club, AC Milan.
explanation: Maldini never played for another club in a professional career spanning over two decades.
